meta_title: For Freelancers: project management to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Freelancers: Solving No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Stop letting a lack of design skills stall your SaaS builds. Master this project management framework using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship like a pro.
content.answer_block: You don't need a designer; you need an architectural system. By utilizing structured project management, you can force-multiply your output. Use Antigravity to generate clean, component-based UI structures and Claude Code to iterate on them instantly, ensuring your code remains modular, scalable, and visually polished without manual design efforts.
content.intro:
The blank editor is a graveyard for ambitious ideas. You have the logic, the backend, and the drive, but every time you face a UI component, the progress bar hits zero. You aren't failing at coding; you're failing at the friction of design. It’s time to stop treating design as a separate, agonizing phase and start treating it as a managed engineering constraint.

We are shifting the paradigm from 'designing' to 'assembling.' By using Antigravity to scaffold your architectural components and Claude Code to maintain the implementation loop, you turn design into a repeatable, automated process. This is how you reclaim your velocity and deliver high-fidelity SaaS products that look like they were built by a dedicated design team.

steps.title: Define the Atomic Design System
steps.description: Establish a rigid UI foundation before coding. This prevents feature creep and design indecision.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Frontend Architect. Create a directory structure for a React/Tailwind project following an atomic design pattern. Define the base button, input, and card components as modular, reusable files.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create the directory structure for /components following atomic design principles.'

steps.title: Automate Component Scaffold
steps.description: Generate boilerplate code for your core components to ensure consistency across the entire application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a highly reusable, accessible Button component using Tailwind CSS and Lucide icons. Include variants for primary, secondary, and ghost styles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Scaffold a Button.tsx component with variants and prop types using the established design tokens.'

steps.title: Implement Layout Constraints
steps.description: Create a layout wrapper that enforces spacing and alignment, removing the need for manual 'pixel pushing'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a responsive grid layout system using CSS Grid that enforces 8px spacing increments throughout the app.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Apply a global layout wrapper to the App entry point that enforces consistent padding and max-width constraints.'

steps.title: Iterative Refinement Loop
steps.description: Use Claude Code to review your generated UI against the design tokens you established in step 1.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this code for common design inconsistencies and suggest a CSS configuration file that locks down the design system.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Review all components in /components and ensure they adhere to the design tokens in tailwind.config.js.'

steps.title: Final Quality Audit
steps.description: Perform a final pass to ensure your project management system is reflected in the codebase structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a checklist for verifying component accessibility and responsiveness across mobile, tablet, and desktop views.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Run a linting and accessibility audit on all generated components and fix any identified issues.'

pro_tips: Always define a global tailwind.config.js before touching a single component file to force design constraints.
Use Claude Code to 'comment out' design debt by asking it to refactor messy inline styles into reusable classes.
Treat your UI components as data structures; if you can't map over the data, the component isn't finished.
Leverage Antigravity to generate documentation for your components simultaneously with the code.
Never manually write a color hex code; use CSS variables defined in your root file for instant theme switching.
content.next_steps: Once your components are locked, integrate a headless CMS to decouple your content from the UI, further speeding up your development cycle.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting design friction kill your SaaS dreams. Master the workflow that separates the hobbyists from the professional ship-first builders. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
